pi_items.source: Task description explicitly lists these three websites: 'Wikipedia Main Page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page), Craigslist (https://craigslist.org), and the Internet Archive (https://archive.org)'
Verified across setup_task.sh (line 44), export_result.sh (line 18), and Python analysis script (line 70). Directory creation confirmed in setup_task.sh line 43.
Task description explicitly states 'Document at least 2 specific issues per site' for each of the three sites
Task requires 2 issues minimum per site × 3 sites = 6 total minimum. Verifier.py line 110 confirms this with comment '2 per site * 3'. The verifier checks wcag_refs_valid >= 6 for full points, not 8.

Regex pattern verified against WCAG 2.1 criterion format. Pattern correctly matches valid criteria (1.1.1, 4.1.3, etc.) and rejects invalid patterns (5.1.1, 0.1.1, 1.1.A, etc.). Consistent with export_result.sh line 93.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires auditing three real websites (Wikipedia Main Page at en.wikipedia.org, Craigslist at craigslist.org, and Internet Archive at archive.org) using Firefox's Accessibility Inspector. Findings must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/accessibility_audit.json in JSON format with audit metadata and issues. A minimum of 2 specific accessibility issues per site must be documented with valid WCAG 2.1 criterion references (format: X.X.X where X is 1-4 for the first digit). This yields a minimum of 6 total issues across the three sites. The websites are real public sites accessed during task execution, not synthetic data.
